messages.content: You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the filing deadline for amended Form 1000 submissions?
Amended Form 1000 certificates must be submitted to the withholding agent by February 1 of the following year.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Who is required to file Form 1000?

U.S. citizens, resident individuals, fiduciaries, partnerships, or nonresident partnerships (with all members being citizens/residents) who own bonds containing a tax-free covenant issued before January 1, 1934 must file Form 1000.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What type of bonds require Form 1000 for interest payments?
Form 1000 is required for interest payments on bonds that have a tax-free covenant and were issued before January 1, 1934 by a domestic or foreign corporation.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where should Form 1000 be filed?
Form 1000 must be filed with the withholding agent for interest payments on qualifying bonds, not directly with the IRS.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What form should be used to summarize Form 1000 data for the IRS?
Withholding agents must use Form 1042 to summarize the information from Form 1000 for submission to the IRS.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What information is required on Line 1(a) of Form 1000?
Line 1(a) should indicate 'Individual, estate, or trust, whose taxable income exceeds the deductions for exemptions' for applicable cases.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the retention period for Form 1000?
Form 1000 must be retained for at least 4 years after the end of the last calendar year in which the income to which the form applies is paid.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should be entered on Line 7 of Form 1000?
Line 7 requires the signature of the owner, fiduciary, trustee, or agent completing the form.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the 'tax-free covenant' referenced on Form 1000?
A 'tax-free covenant' refers to a bond provision that ensures certain interest payments are not subject to federal income tax, as specified in the IRS regulations for pre-1934 bonds.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Can a single Form 1000 cover multiple bond issues?
No, a separate Form 1000 must be completed for each distinct issue of bonds.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Regarding Form 1000: What information must be included in the 'Classes of Bond Owners' section?
The 'Classes of Bond Owners' section requires specifying the owner type (e.g., individual with income exceeding deductions) and corresponding tax calculations.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the purpose of the OMB control number on Form 1000?
The OMB control number (1545-0054) indicates the form has been approved under the Paperwork Reduction Act to collect information for tax administration purposes.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Regarding Form 1000: What happens if a nonresident foreign corporation issues the bonds?
If a nonresident foreign corporation issues the bonds, Form 1000 must include the name and address of both the U.S. fiscal/paying agent and the nonresident debtor corporation.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the estimated time to prepare and file Form 1000?
The estimated average time is 9 minutes for form preparation, plus 6 minutes to learn about the law and 3 hours 6 minutes for recordkeeping.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the consequence of not providing the U.S. identifying number on Form 1000?
Failure to provide the U.S. identifying number may result in rejection of the form by the withholding agent, as it is a required field for tax administration.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Regarding Form 1000: What is the tax rate applied to interest in the 'Tax Assumed' section?
The 'Tax Assumed' section indicates a 2% tax rate is applied to the gross amount of interest paid for qualifying bondholders.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Regarding Form 1000: What is the purpose of the 'Name of bond and date of issue' field?
This field identifies the specific bond issue and its original issuance date for tax reporting purposes.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where should nonresident partnerships with U.S. citizen members file Form 1000?
Nonresident partnerships where all members are citizens/residents must file Form 1000 with the withholding agent for their qualifying bond interest payments.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the deadline for submitting Form 1000 to the IRS?
Form 1000 is not submitted to the IRS. It must be filed with the withholding agent by February 1 of the following year if amended, or as needed for interest payments.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Regarding Form 1000: What is the 'Gross Amount of Interest Paid' field used for?
This field reports the total interest paid on the bond for tax calculation purposes, which is then used to determine the 2% assumed tax.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the submission method for Form 1024?
Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where can I access the most recent version of Form 1024?
The most recent version of Form 1024 can be accessed at Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
How do I file Form 1024 electronically?
Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the official website for Form 1024 information?
The official website for Form 1024 information is http://www.irs.gov/Form1024.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Which platform is required for Form 1024 submission?
Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where should I go to find Form 1024 details?
You can find Form 1024 details at http://www.irs.gov/Form1024.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
How do I obtain the latest Form 1024?
The latest version of Form 1024 can be accessed at Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the required method for Form 1024 filing?
Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where is the official Form 1024 available?
The official Form 1024 is available at Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

The current version of Form 1024 can be accessed at Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Instructions for Form 1024 can be found at http://www.irs.gov/Form1024.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Form 1024 must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.

You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the filing deadline for Form 1023-EZ?
The document does not specify a filing deadline for Form 1023-EZ. Filers must check the IRS website for current deadlines.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
How do I complete Part II of Form 1023-EZ?
Part II requires selecting your organization type (corporation, unincorporated association, or trust) and attesting to having necessary organizing documents.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Who is required to file Form 1023-EZ?
Organizations seeking recognition of exemption under section 501(c)(3) must file Form 1023-EZ after completing the Eligibility Worksheet.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 7 of Form 1023-EZ?
Line 7 of Form 1023-EZ should indicate whether a user fee was submitted for the application.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What does 'Form 1023-EZ' consider as qualified expenses?
Qualified expenses are defined in the instructions and include costs directly related to the organization's exempt purposes, such as program services and administrative costs.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where must Form 1023-EZ be filed?
Form 1023-EZ must be filed electronically exclusively on Pay.gov, as stated in the document.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the purpose of Part III on Form 1023-EZ?
Part III asks about the organization's specific activities, including NTEE code selection and attestation to exempt purposes under section 501(c)(3).
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Regarding Form 1023-EZ: How do I determine my organization's NTEE code?
The NTEE code is a 3-character code that best describes your organization's activities, as specified in the instructions for Form 1023-EZ.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What must be attested to in Part II, Line 2 of Form 1023-EZ?
Part II, Line 2 requires attesting that the organization has the necessary organizing documents for its structure.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the significance of Part IV on Form 1023-EZ?
Part IV classifies the organization as either a private foundation or public charity, with public charity status offering more favorable tax treatment.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is required for Part IV, Line 1a of Form 1023-EZ?
Line 1a of Part IV requires attesting that the organization receives at least one-third of its support from public sources to qualify as a public charity.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the role of the Eligibility Worksheet for Form 1023-EZ?
The Eligibility Worksheet helps determine if an organization meets the requirements to file Form 1023-EZ for exemption recognition.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What must be attested to in Part III, Line 3 of Form 1023-EZ?
Part III, Line 3 requires attesting that the organization refrains from supporting or opposing candidates in political campaigns.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the purpose of Part V on Form 1023-EZ?
Part V is for organizations seeking reinstatement of exemption after automatic revocation due to failure to file required returns.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What must be attested to in Part VI of Form 1023-EZ?
Part VI requires a declaration under penalty of perjury that all information provided is true and accurate.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the requirement for dissolution provisions in Form 1023-EZ?
The organization must attest that its organizing document includes dissolution provisions ensuring assets are used exclusively for exempt purposes.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the requirement for unrelated business income in Form 1023-EZ?
The organization must attest it does not have unrelated business gross income of $1,000 or more during a tax year.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What must be attested to in Part III, Line 6 of Form 1023-EZ?
Part III, Line 6 requires attesting that the organization does not provide commercial-type insurance as a substantial part of its activities.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the requirement for financial transactions with insiders in Form 1023-EZ?
The organization must attest it does not engage in financial transactions with officers, directors, or entities they control.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the definition of 'compensation' in Form 1023-EZ?
Compensation refers to payments made to officers, directors, or trustees for services rendered, as defined in the instructions.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What must be attested to in Part III, Line 4 of Form 1023-EZ?
Part III, Line 4 requires attesting that the organization does not attempt to influence legislation or exceed section 501(h) expenditure limits.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What must be attested to in Part III, Line 5 of Form 1023-EZ?
Part III, Line 5 requires attesting that the organization does not donate funds to individuals or make payments for their expenses.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the required method for submitting Form 1024-A?
Form 1024-A must be submitted electronically through Pay.gov.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Where can I access the most recent version of Form 1024-A?
The most recent version of Form 1024-A is available at Pay.gov.

How do I obtain Form 1024-A?
You can access Form 1024-A by visiting Pay.gov.

For more information about Form 1024-A, visit www.irs.gov/Form1024a.

What is the new mailing address for Form 1028?

The new mailing address for Form 1028 is Internal Revenue Service TE/GE Stop 31A Team 105 P.O. Box 12192 Covington, KY 41012–0192. Deliveries by private delivery service (PDS) should be made to Internal Revenue Service 7940 Kentucky Drive TE/GE Stop 31A Team 105 Florence, KY 41042.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
Who is required to file Form 1028?

Form 1028 is required for organizations seeking recognition of exemption under Section 521 of the Internal Revenue Code. However, if the organization does not have an organizing document, it must not file this application.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 7a of Form 1028?
Line 7a of Form 1028 asks whether state law requires the accumulation and maintenance of reserves. If 'Yes,' you must state the names and purposes of the reserves and enter the amount of each.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
How do I complete Part II of Form 1028?

Part II of Form 1028 requires you to check the applicable entity box (e.g., Corporation—Articles of Incorporation, bylaws) and attach a conformed copy of the organizing and operational documents listed.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the purpose of Form 1028?
Form 1028 is the Application for Recognition of Exemption Under Section 521 of the Internal Revenue Code. It is used by organizations to request exemption from federal income tax.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 1a of Form 1028?
Line 1a of Form 1028 requires you to enter the full name of the organization. This information is specified in the instructions for Part I.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the deadline to file Form 1028?
The document does not specify a filing deadline for Form 1028. Filers should consult the instructions or contact the IRS for specific deadlines related to their situation.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 4 of Form 1028?
Line 4 of Form 1028 requires you to enter the date the organization was incorporated or formed. This information is part of Part I Identification.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 2a of Form 1028?
Line 2a of Form 1028 requires you to enter the number, street, and room or suite number (or P.O. box number if applicable) of the organization's principal address.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 6b of Form 1028?

Line 6b of Form 1028 requires you to state the form numbers, years filed, and the Internal Revenue Service office where the organization filed Federal income tax returns if the answer to Line 6a is 'Yes.'
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 1c of Form 1028?

Line 1c of Form 1028 requires you to enter the number of shares of common stock (voting) currently outstanding, if any, along with the value of the consideration for which they were issued and the rate of dividend paid.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 2d of Form 1028?
Line 2d of Form 1028 requires you to enter the total number of shares owned by producers and nonproducers, calculated by adding lines 2a and 2b.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 2e of Form 1028?
Line 2e of Form 1028 requires you to calculate the percentage owned by current and active producers by dividing line 2c by line 2d.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 7b of Form 1028?
Line 7b of Form 1028 requires you to enter the amount of each reserve if state law requires the accumulation and maintenance of reserves, as stated in Line 7a.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 8b of Form 1028?
Line 8b of Form 1028 requires you to state the names and purposes of any reserves maintained or planned to be maintained other than those required by state law, along with their amounts.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 10b of Form 1028?
Line 10b of Form 1028 asks whether patronage dividends will be paid to all patrons, both members and nonmembers, on the same basis. If 'Yes,' you must specify this.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 11a of Form 1028?

Line 11a of Form 1028 requires you to state whether the allocation of patronage dividends is based on an obligation in existence before the cooperative received the allocated amounts. If 'Yes,' you must specify the source of the obligation.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 12 of Form 1028?
Line 12 of Form 1028 requires you to explain all the activities in which the association is or will be engaged, including any operational details.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 13 of Form 1028?

Line 13 of Form 1028 requires you to explain how distribution of proceeds from products marketed for members and nonmembers will be made, including how losses are treated if the organization operates on an allocated units basis.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 14 of Form 1028?
Line 14 of Form 1028 requires you to explain how the association charges for supplies and equipment bought for members and nonmembers.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 15 of Form 1028?
Line 15 of Form 1028 requires you to explain the requirements for membership in the association, including any criteria or conditions for becoming a member.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 16a of Form 1028?

Line 16a of Form 1028 asks whether all the association’s member cooperatives are exempt under Section 521. If 'No,' you must indicate whether nonexempt member cooperatives have the same annual accounting period as the association.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 16c of Form 1028?

Line 16c of Form 1028 requires you to specify the method used or to be used by the association to provide a common or comparable unit of time for analyzing operations, if nonexempt member cooperatives do not have the same annual accounting period.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What is the revision date for Form 1028?
Form 1028 was revised in September 2006. This date is noted in the document as the revision date.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 3 of Form 1028?
Line 3 of Form 1028 requires you to provide the name and telephone number (including area code) of the person to be contacted during business hours.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.
What should I enter on Line 5 of Form 1028?
Line 5 of Form 1028 requires you to enter the month the organization's annual accounting period ends. This information is part of Part I Identification.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the filing deadline for Form 1040?
The filing deadline for Form 1040 for the 2025 tax year is April 15, 2026.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: Who is required to file Form 1040?
Individuals with taxable income exceeding the standard deduction threshold, or those with certain types of income (e.g., self-employment, investment gains), must file Form 1040.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I complete Part II of Form 1040?
Part II requires reporting income from wages, salaries, tips, and other sources. Enter amounts on lines 1a–1z, then add them to calculate total income.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What should I enter on Line 7 of Form 1040?
Line 7 of Form 1040 is for reporting income from a second job or additional sources not included in other lines.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What does 'Form 1040' consider as qualified expenses?
Form 1040 does not directly define qualified expenses; these are typically specified in schedules like Schedule A for itemized deductions.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report taxable interest on Form 1040?
Report taxable interest on Form 1040, line 2b, using amounts from Form 1099-INT or other applicable statements.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the purpose of Part III on Form 1040?
Part III of Form 1040 is for calculating taxes, credits, and determining your final tax liability or refund.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report capital gains or losses on Form 1040?
Report capital gains or losses on Form 1040, line 7a, and attach Schedule D if required.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the standard deduction for Form 1040 in 2025?
The standard deduction for 2025 varies by filing status: $13,500 for single filers, $27,000 for married filing jointly, and $18,000 for heads of household.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report social security benefits on Form 1040?
Report social security benefits on Form 1040, line 6a, and indicate taxable amounts on line 6b.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the procedure for claiming the child tax credit on Form 1040?
Claim the child tax credit on Form 1040, line 7, by entering the qualifying child’s information on the dependent section.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report IRA distributions on Form 1040?
Report IRA distributions on Form 1040, line 4a, and indicate taxable amounts on line 4b.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What should I do if I received a Form W-2 but did not get a Form 1099-R?
If you received a Form W-2 but not a Form 1099-R, report the missing information on Form 1040, line 1a, and consult the instructions for details.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report combat zone pay on Form 1040?
Report combat zone pay on Form 1040, line 1i, and indicate it is nontaxable if you elected the combat zone exclusion.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the procedure for reporting dependent care benefits on Form 1040?
Report dependent care benefits on Form 1040, line 1e, using amounts from Form 2441, line 26.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report pensions and annuities on Form 1040?
Report pensions and annuities on Form 1040, line 5a, and indicate taxable amounts on line 5b.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What should I enter on Line 1z of Form 1040?
Line 1z of Form 1040 is for adding all income amounts from lines 1a through 1h to calculate total income.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report taxable dividends on Form 1040?
Report ordinary dividends on Form 1040, line 3b, and qualified dividends on line 3a.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the purpose of Schedule B on Form 1040?
Schedule B is used to report additional income, such as rents, royalties, or other taxable income not included in Part I.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report wages from self-employment on Form 1040?
Report self-employment wages on Form 1040, line 1a, and include any additional income on line 1h.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What should I do if I have more than four dependents on Form 1040?
If you have more than four dependents, check the 'Dependents' section on Form 1040 and complete additional dependent lines as needed.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report adoption benefits on Form 1040?
Report employer-provided adoption benefits on Form 1040, line 1f, using amounts from Form 8839, line 31.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: What is the procedure for claiming the dependent care credit on Form 1040?
Claim the dependent care credit on Form 1040, line 1e, by entering the amount from Form 2441, line 26.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form
1040: How do I report Medicaid waiver payments on Form 1040?
Report Medicaid waiver payments on Form 1040, line 1d, if not reported on Form(s) W-2.
You are an IRS tax documentation expert.

Regarding Form

What is the purpose of Form 1040-C?
Form 1040-C is used by U.S. departuring aliens to report income tax for the tax period or year they reside in the U.S. before leaving.

What is the filing deadline for Form 1040-C?
The document does not specify a filing deadline for Form 1040-C. Refer to the IRS website for the latest information.

Who is required to file Form 1040-C?
Form 1040-C is required for U.S. departuring aliens who have income from U.S. sources during their stay and must file a final return after their tax year ends.

How do I complete Part II of Form 1040-C?
Part II requires listing dependents with their names, social security numbers, relationship to you, and qualification for credits like the child tax credit.
